# Olympia Treasury — Protocol-Controlled Vault for Ethereum Classic

> The Olympia Treasury is an immutable, protocol-controlled vault that receives EIP-1559 basefee revenue and disburses funds through on-chain governance. Non-inflationary, transparent, and auditable.

## What is the Olympia Treasury?

The Olympia Treasury (ECIP-1112) receives basefee revenue from every transaction on Ethereum Classic via EIP-1559, voluntary donations, and mining rewards directed to the treasury address. No new ETC coins are minted — all funds come from existing transaction fees. All withdrawals require on-chain governance approval.

## How Funds Flow

1. **BaseFee Collected** — After Olympia activates, EIP-1559 basefee from every block is redirected to the treasury by consensus.
2. **Mined Income** — Mining pools and solo miners can direct block rewards and tx fees to the treasury by mining to the contract address.
3. **Direct Donations** — Anyone can send ETC directly to the treasury contract to support protocol development transparently.
4. **Treasury Accumulates** — All three income streams grow the vault. Balance and full history are publicly visible on-chain.
5. **Proposals Submitted** — Contributors submit ECFPs specifying recipient, amount, and milestones for governance review.
6. **Governance Approves** — DAO votes and authorizes eligible proposals. Only governance can trigger fund releases.

## Core Invariants

- No Minting — Cannot mint ETC, only holds received inflows
- Immutable Code — No proxy patterns, no admin methods
- Protocol-Controlled — Owned by protocol rules, not a multisig
- Minimal Interface — Accepts deposits, executes on governance signal
- Fully Transparent — All inflows and outflows visible on-chain

## Security Model

- Protocol Consensus — Client implementations enforce treasury rules at the protocol level
- Contract Immutability — No upgradeable proxy, no admin methods
- Sanctions Defense — OFAC screening at proposal level + emergency pause

## Governance Stages

1. Accumulate — BaseFee + mining + donations (active)
2. Govern — On-chain proposals and voting
3. Fund — Treasury disbursements
4. Predict — Futarchy governance
5. Optimize — Protocol improvements

## Fund Allocation Categories

- Developer Retention
- Infrastructure Maintenance
- Emergency Response
- Public Goods

## Deployed Contracts (Demo v0.2)

Seven contracts deployed via deterministic CREATE2 — identical addresses on Mordor Testnet (chain 63) and ETC Mainnet (chain 61):

- OlympiaTreasury — Protocol-controlled vault (0x035b2e3c189B772e52F4C3DA6c45c84A3bB871bf)
- OlympiaGovernor — On-chain governance (0xB85dbc899472756470EF4033b9637ff8fa2FD23D)
- OlympiaExecutor — Proposal execution (0x64624f74F77639CbA268a6c8bEDC2778B707eF9a)
- TimelockController — Security timelock (0xA5839b3e9445f7eE7AffdBC796DC0601f9b976C2)
- ECFPRegistry — Proposal registry (0xFB4De5674a6b9a301d16876795a74f3bdacfa722)
- OlympiaMemberNFT — Soulbound membership (0x73e78d3a3470396325b975FcAFA8105A89A9E672)
- SanctionsOracle — Compliance screening (0xfF2B8D7937D908D81C72D20AC99302EE6ACc2709)

## Web Properties

- olympiatreasury.org — This site (treasury dashboard)
- olympiadao.org — Governance landing page
- app.olympiadao.org — Proposal submission, voting, and execution
- ethereumclassicdao.org — Institutional (Ethereum Classic DAO LLC)
- github.com/olympiadao — Open-source repositories
